title: Chamaedorea fragrans
taxonomy: Chamaedorea fragrans is a species of palm tree that belongs to the family Arecaceae. This classification places it within the order Arecales, which comprises all palm species. The genus Chamaedorea is a group of evergreen palms, with Chamaedorea fragrans being one of the 150 species within this genus. Chamaedorea fragrans is native to Central and South America, where it grows in tropical regions. The specific classification of Chamaedorea fragrans as Chamaedorea seifrizii (Synonym) within Arecaceae family is still in use.
uses: Chamaedorea fragrans is a species of palm tree, but information on its medicinal uses is limited. Chamaedorea fragrans is often confused with other species within the same genus.
